release_review.summary: Flower adds a /compact thread action, typed live timeline anchors, and Desktop Local UI routing without changing the Runtime Service compatibility window.
checked_surfaces: Runtime Service protocol_version remains redeven-runtime-v1; Flower /compact is exposed through existing Local UI and Env App RPC surfaces rather than a runtime-service protocol version change
Env App redeven_v1 ai.compactThreadContext request/response codecs, SDK, and wire type ids were regenerated and tested as Env App UI protocol assets

Desktop Local Environment Flower adapter and main-process runtime request allowlist include /threads/:thread_id/context/compact while preserving startup reports, attach flows, sys.ping, and Local UI health checks

Flower live context compaction events use the single context.compaction.updated event with typed timeline anchors; old anchor_message_id plus placement and started/applied event shapes are rejected by protocol checks
Flower composer /compact command does not create transcript messages, does not stop a running or waiting-approval run, and carries active_run_id only as the active-run identity guard
Floret is consumed only from the published github.com/floegence/floret v0.3.43 module with GOWORK=off validation and no replace, go.work, package-manager link, or local sibling path wiring
Flower post-compaction continuation keeps active runs and queued follow-ups on Redeven thread actors while Floret owns compact lifecycle cancellation and diagnostic observation

Compatibility epoch, minimum Desktop version, and minimum Runtime version remain unchanged because Desktop-managed startup, runtime snapshot protocol, SSH bootstrap, gateway lifecycle, and Runtime Service control methods are not expanded
release_review.same_window_rationale: The compatibility window is unchanged because the feature is implemented inside Flower, Env App UI protocol assets, and Desktop Local UI request routing. The Runtime Service snapshot protocol remains redeven-runtime-v1, Desktop-managed startup still uses structured machine presentation and startup reports, sys.ping and attach semantics are unchanged, and SSH/runtime bootstrap compatibility does not require a newer Desktop or Runtime binary pair.
